events.label: assistant
events.text: Assessment complete — this is a **fail**. Solvent-welding ABS to PVC with the wrong (single-material) cement doesn't create a code-recognized joint: ABS cement (ASTM D2235) and PVC cement (ASTM D2564) aren't interchangeable across the dissimilar resins, and IPC 705.10.3 requires either an approved mechanical transition coupling or a manufacturer-listed transition cement system for ABS-to-PVC joints. Since this is at rough-in and about to be concealed, I flagged it high risk and called for cutting out the joint and redoing it with a listed coupling or cement before close-in. Full findings/actions/references are in `/app/answer.json`.
